document.purpose: Vision, outcomes, and transformation strategy
SDK to OMNISKILL capability mapping with implementation intent
Target architecture, contracts, and state machines
Workstreams, dependencies, acceptance gates, and prompts
Quality validation and stress verification strategy
Security, policy, and governance controls
Migration, rollout, and release operations
Claude-optimized prompting, context control, and execution protocol
Epic-level executable backlog with dependencies and definition-of-done
Ready-to-run prompt templates per workstream and phase
Formal context lifecycle, handoff packet schema, and compression policy
Release-readiness scorecard and hard stop/go criteria
Schema and CI quality enforcement model
Capability-to-epic-to-test-to-gate traceability
Automation model for issue generation and dashboard ops
Golden-run baselines and adversarial red-team validation pack
Governance operating model and accountability cadence
STRIDE threat model and mapped defenses
Preventive, detective, corrective control catalog
SLO/SLA commitments and error budget policy
Performance and reliability benchmark framework
Model routing and reasoning-effort governance
Architectural decision record framework
Operational self-healing and incident playbooks
Quantitative evaluation rubric for plan quality
Capacity and cost optimization planning
Retention and provenance governance
Controlled experimentation and change policy

recommendedReadOrder.step: Read 01 and 08 first to align strategy and execution behavior.
Read 02 and 03 to align capability mapping and architecture contracts.
Read 04 and 09 to execute implementation with dependency awareness.
Use 10 and 11 continuously for prompt quality and context stability.
Use 05, 06, and 12 as non-negotiable quality and release gates.
Use 07 for migration and rollout finalization.

executionDirectives.directive: Use OMNISKILL v2 as the mandatory implementation baseline for all v3 execution tasks.
Extend v2 primitives before introducing net-new abstractions unless a blocker is documented.
Enforce tool-first workflows and context-budget rules to prevent transcript flooding.
